prompt: Create a premium ultra-realistic studio portrait of the same toddler duplicated exactly 6 times, each clone having the exact same face, curly medium-brown hair, warm light skin tone, eye color, cheerful smile, body proportions, and identical outfit. The toddler is a cute 2-year-old boy wearing a beige ribbed long-sleeve henley sweater with small buttons, tan chinos, and clean white sneakers. Arrange the clones around a tall vertical stack of oversized matte white 3D block letters spelling OSCAR, one letter per row from top to bottom. Use a warm beige seamless studio backdrop and floor, soft diffused professional lighting, gentle shadows, and a polished editorial kids-fashion look. Composition: vertical 2:3 portrait. Count and placement of the 6 toddler clones: 1 sitting on top of the top letter O with one leg bent and one leg extended; 2 climbing or hanging from the upper right side between O and S, smiling toward camera; 3 peeking from the left side around the S with hands on the letter; 4 seated on the left side beside the C/A area with legs forward; 5 lying/crawling at the bottom left in front of the R with hands on the floor; 6 standing full-body on the right side, one hand touching the C, smiling directly at camera. Make the white letters thick, rounded, sculptural, foam-like or painted wood, with realistic depth and soft shadows. Keep the toddler identity perfectly consistent across all 6 duplicates, with no facial variations and no outfit changes. Avoid extra text, props, logos, watermarks, distorted anatomy, mismatched faces, or additional children.
